title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Practice Set 9: Shapes and Counting
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Mia drew some stars on her notebook. How many stars did Mia draw in total?
questions.image_description: Five stars drawn in a row.
questions.solution: Counting each star one by one gives 5 stars total.

questions.question_text: Ben is looking at a shape on the wall. How many sides does this shape have?
questions.solution: A pentagon has 5 sides.

questions.question_text: Leo has 3 red apples and 2 green apples. How many apples does Leo have in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='50' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='80' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='130' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='160' cy='30' r='12'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three circles on the left and two circles on the right.
questions.solution: 3 + 2 = 5 apples total.

questions.question_text: Sara is making a pattern: circle, square, circle, square, circle. What comes next in Sara's pattern?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 250 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='20' cy='25' r='12'/><rect x='40' y='13' width='24' height='24'/><circle cx='90' cy='25' r='12'/><rect x='110' y='13' width='24' height='24'/><circle cx='160' cy='25' r='12'/><text x='195' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Alternating pattern of circles and squares with a question mark at the end.
questions.solution: The pattern alternates circle and square, so after circle comes square.

questions.question_text: Tom woke up when the clock showed this time. What time did Tom wake up?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='50' y2='20' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='70' y2='50' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='46' y='18' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='8'>12</text><text x='85' y='53' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='8'>3</text><text x='46' y='92' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='8'>6</text><text x='10' y='53' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='8'>9</text></svg>
questions.solution: The hour hand points to 3 and minute hand points to 12, so it is 3 o'clock.

questions.question_text: Anna has 4 pencils and Kim has 7 pencils. Who has more pencils, Anna or Kim?
questions.solution: 7 is greater than 4, so Kim has more pencils.

questions.question_text: Emma painted some triangles on a page for art class. How many triangles did Emma paint?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><polygon points='20,50 35,20 50,50'/><polygon points='70,50 85,20 100,50'/><polygon points='120,50 135,20 150,50'/><polygon points='170,50 185,20 200,50'/></svg>
questions.solution: Counting each triangle gives 4 triangles.

questions.question_text: Jake had 8 marbles in his pocket. He gave 3 marbles to his friend Ray. How many marbles does Jake have now?
questions.solution: 8 - 3 = 5 marbles left.

questions.question_text: Nora drew a figure using squares. How many squares are there in the figure Nora drew?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='90' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='20' height='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A row of 5 squares with one extra square below the second one.
questions.solution: There are 5 squares in the top row plus 1 below, totaling 6 squares.

questions.question_text: Zoe has a box with a red ball, a blue ball, and a green ball. If Zoe takes out the blue ball, which balls stay in the box?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='30' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='80' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='130' cy='30' r='15'/><text x='22' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>R</text><text x='72' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>B</text><text x='122' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>G</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three balls labeled R, B, and G.
questions.solution: Removing the blue ball leaves the red and green balls in the box.

questions.question_text: Olivia bought 6 cookies from the bakery. Her brother Max ate 2 and Olivia ate 1. How many cookies are left?
questions.solution: 6 - 2 - 1 = 3 cookies left.

questions.question_text: Felix writes numbers in order: 2, 4, 6, 8, and then one more. What number should Felix write next?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 40' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><text x='10' y='25' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='16'>2, 4, 6, 8, ?</text></svg>
questions.solution: The pattern adds 2 each time, so after 8 comes 10.

questions.question_text: Lily sees some birds and some butterflies in the garden. How many butterflies does Lily see?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><ellipse cx='30' cy='30' rx='10' ry='6'/><ellipse cx='60' cy='30' rx='10' ry='6'/><ellipse cx='90' cy='30' rx='10' ry='6'/><circle cx='140' cy='40' r='6'/><circle cx='152' cy='40' r='6'/><circle cx='180' cy='40' r='6'/><circle cx='192' cy='40' r='6'/><circle cx='220' cy='40' r='6'/><circle cx='232' cy='40' r='6'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three bird shapes on the left and three butterfly pairs on the right.
questions.solution: Counting the butterfly pairs on the right gives 3 butterflies.

questions.question_text: Ethan draws a circle inside a square on his paper. Which figure matches what Ethan drew?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 80 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='10' width='60' height='60'/><circle cx='40' cy='40' r='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A circle drawn inside a square.
questions.solution: The figure shows a circle inside a square.

questions.question_text: Ruby has 5 stickers in her album. Ivy has 2 more stickers than Ruby. How many stickers does Ivy have?
questions.solution: Ivy has 5 + 2 = 7 stickers.

questions.question_text: Noah has 3 hats: one red, one yellow, and one blue. He wears the red hat on Monday. How many hats does Noah have left to wear this week?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><polygon points='20,50 40,20 60,50'/><polygon points='80,50 100,20 120,50'/><polygon points='140,50 160,20 180,50'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three triangle hats in a row.
questions.solution: 3 - 1 = 2 hats remaining to wear.

questions.question_text: Grace has 4 boxes and each box contains exactly 2 toys inside. How many toys does Grace have in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='110' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='160' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><text x='22' y='35' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='14'>2</text><text x='72' y='35' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='14'>2</text><text x='122' y='35' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='14'>2</text><text x='172' y='35' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='14'>2</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Four boxes, each containing the number 2.
questions.solution: 4 boxes times 2 toys per box equals 8 toys total.

questions.question_text: Henry made a large triangle using small triangles for a puzzle. How many small triangles can you count in Henry's big triangle?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 110' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><polygon points='60,10 10,100 110,100'/><line x1='35' y1='55' x2='85' y2='55' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='35' y2='55' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='85' y2='55' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle divided into 4 smaller triangles.
questions.solution: The big triangle is divided into 4 small triangles.

questions.question_text: Alex had some candies at the start. He gave 3 to Sam and 2 to Lin, and now Alex has 4 candies left. How many candies did Alex have at the start?
questions.solution: 4 + 3 + 2 = 9 candies at the start.

questions.question_text: Maya stacks blocks following a pattern. The rows from top to bottom have 1, 2, 3, and 4 blocks. How many blocks did Maya use in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='40' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='20' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='40' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='60' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='70' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='70' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='70' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='70' y='70' width='20' height='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A triangle-shaped stack with rows of 1, 2, 3, and 4 blocks.
questions.solution: 1 + 2 + 3 + 4 = 10 blocks in total.

questions.question_text: Chloe, Dan, and Eve are standing in a line. Chloe is first, Eve is last. Who is in the middle of the line?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 180 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='30' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='90' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='150' cy='30' r='15'/><text x='22' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>C</text><text x='85' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>?</text><text x='142' y='34' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>E</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three circles in a line labeled C, question mark, E.
questions.solution: Since Chloe is first and Eve is last, Dan must be in the middle.

questions.question_text: Lucas starts reading his adventure book at 2 o'clock and reads for 3 hours straight. At what time does Lucas finish reading?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='50' y2='20' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='70' y2='40' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='46' y='18' stroke='none' fill='currentColor' font-size='8'>12</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A clock face showing the start time of 2 o'clock.
questions.solution: 2 + 3 = 5, so Lucas finishes at 5 o'clock.

questions.question_text: Jade looks at a bookshelf with 3 shelves, and each shelf has exactly 3 books. How many books does Jade see?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 150 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='20' y='10' width='15' height='25'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='15' height='25'/><rect x='60' y='10' width='15' height='25'/><line x1='10' y1='40' x2='130' y2='40' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='20' y='50' width='15' height='25'/><rect x='40' y='50' width='15' height='25'/><rect x='60' y='50' width='15' height='25'/><line x1='10' y1='80' x2='130' y2='80' stroke='currentColor'/><rect x='20' y='90' width='15' height='25'/><rect x='40' y='90' width='15' height='25'/><rect x='60' y='90' width='15' height='25'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A bookshelf with 3 shelves, each holding 3 books.
questions.solution: 3 shelves times 3 books per shelf equals 9 books.

questions.question_text: Owen has 10 coins in his jar. Piper has 3 fewer coins than Owen. Quinn has 2 more coins than Piper. How many coins does Quinn have?
questions.solution: Piper has 10 - 3 = 7 coins. Quinn has 7 + 2 = 9 coins.
